Home / Alternatives to Monday.com / Monday.com vs Focalboard

Monday.com vs Focalboard

A side-by-side look at Monday.com (the paid SaaS) and Focalboard (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Monday.com Focalboard
Tagline Work OS with flexible boards and automations. Open source Trello/Notion hybrid by Mattermost.
License Proprietary SaaS MIT / AGPL-3.0
Pricing Free trial; Basic from $9/user/month (3-seat minimum). Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Windows, macOS, Linux
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

If Monday felt bloated and you want something simpler.

Focalboard strengths

  • Integrates tightly with Mattermost.
  • Personal desktop mode (no server required).
  • Multiple views — board, table, gallery.

Focalboard weaknesses

  • Development pace has slowed after Mattermost integration.
  • Smaller community.
  • Feature set is narrower than Notion.

What's the catch with Monday.com?

  • Minimum-seat pricing inflates costs for small teams.
  • Can feel bloated.
  • No self-hosted option.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Monday.com: see Monday.com alternatives, or learn more about Focalboard on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
